description: Simplify fractures. Fractures are sorted by decreasing size and, starting with the largest fracture (termed the reference fracture), the algorithm determines smaller fractures in which the following 2 criteria are met:

keywords.name: angle
keywords.description: Angle criterion for fracture proximity identification with f ≥ 0.

keywords.name: collapse
keywords.description: Collapse rotated fractures onto the reference fracture plane. Fractures are not merged.

keywords.name: distance
keywords.description: Distance criterion for fracture proximity identification with f ≥ 0.

keywords.name: merge
keywords.description: Merge fractures that would overlap the reference fractures if they were collapsed onto the reference fracture plane. If a fracture falls completely within a larger fracture, it is removed.

keywords.name: angle
keywords.description: Angle criterion for fracture proximity identification with f ≥ 0.

keywords.name: merge-convex
keywords.description: Collapse rotated fractures onto the reference fracture plane. Fractures are not merged by default. Merge the collapsed fractures into planar convex fractures. This operation happens after all fractures have been collapsed. Collapsed fractures that are within the distance specified with the distance keyword are removed and the convex hull of these fractures is created. This is an iterative operation that, by default, continues until no fractures can be merged unless the iterations keyword is given.

keywords.name: distance
keywords.description: Distance criterion for fracture proximity identification with f ≥ 0.

keywords.name: merge
keywords.description: Merge fractures that would overlap the reference fractures if they were collapsed onto the reference fracture plane. If a fracture falls completely within a larger fracture, it is removed.

This is an iterative operation that, by default, continues until no fractures can be merged unless the iterations keyword is given.
